questions.question: How many distinct ways can you choose a committee of 3 people out of a pool of 10 candidates if order does not matter?
What mathematical principle states that if n items are put into m containers, with n > m, then at least one container must contain more than one item?
In graph theory, what is a path called that visits every single *edge* in a graph exactly once?
What is the base case value usually evaluated for the empty set's power set cardinality? (e.g., how many subsets does an empty set have?)
According to the Handshaking Lemma, the sum of degrees of all vertices in any undirected graph is always equal to what value?
answers: Twice the total number of edges
The square of the number of edges
The number of edges minus one
questions.correctAnswer: Twice the total number of edges

questions.question: What is the truth value of the implication 'P implies Q' if P is false and Q is false?
Which theorem allows you to expand integer powers of a binomial algebraic expression easily using coefficients from Pascal's Triangle?
A tree graph with exactly 50 vertices must contain how many edges?
What mathematical sequence starts with 0 and 1, where each subsequent number is the sum of the previous two?
What relation property is satisfied if for all a and b, whenever a relates to b and b relates to c, then a relates to c?
